publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-11-18 00:14:09 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Organizational records, printed materials, legal documents, CDs, DVDs, and Videos.
did.abstract: Pamphlets provided to soldiers, videos and photos of national dinners organized to support the repeal of Don't Ask Don't Tell, and other posters and records of the organization.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: Since 1993 , OutServe-Servicemembers Legal Defense Network (SLDN) has represented the United States Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual and Transgender (LGBT) community worldwide. OutServe-SLDN is a non-partisan, non-profit (501-c-3), legal services, watchdog and policy organization that provides free and direct legal assistance to service members and veterans affected by the repealed "Don't Ask, Don't Tell" (DADT) law, the current ban on open authentic transgender service and those currently serving who may experience harassment or discrimination. OutServe-SLDN is the heir to the legacy of providing legal services to service members and veterans. From representing over 12,000 service members during the era of DADT to leading the fight to repeal DADT, OutServe-SLDN has been on the frontlines for over 20 years and currently has over 75,000 followers worldwide and over 7,000 active members serving and leading more than 80 chapters around the globe.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Pamphlets provided to soldiers, videos and photos of national dinners organized to support the repeal of DADT, and other posters and records of the organization and its efforts to repeal the Don't Ask Don't Tell policy.

emph.ref: Series III. Research, Official Reports, and SLDN Administrative Files
Series IV. Media, Posters and Awards

list.head: Books - cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
list.item: Boushka, Bill Our Fundamental Rights And How We Can Reclaim Them: A Psychological Approach, 1998
Carey, John J., Editor The Christian Argument for Gays and Lesbians in the Military, 1993
Coye, Beth F. My Navy Too, 1998 (signed)
Drenner, Karla and Paul Clere One: A True Story of Politics, Prayer, and the Power of One, 2002
Dyer, Kate, Editor Gays in Uniform: The Pentagon's Secret Reports, 1990
Humphrey, Mary Ann My Country, My Right To Serve,1990
Johnson, William Stacy A Time to Embrace, 2006
Kennedy, Claudia J. and Malcolm McConnell Generally Speaking: A Memoir By The First Woman Promoted To Three-Star General In the United States Army, 2001
Ludmer, Christopher Trail of the Giant, 2004
Mohr, Richard D. A More Perfect Union: Why Straight America Must Stand Up For Gay Rights, 1994
People For The American Way Foundation Hostile Climate: Report on Anti-Gay Activity, 1999
People For The American Way Foundation Hostile Climate: Report on Anti-Gay Activity, 1998
Swann, Thomas A. The Tom Swann Story: For A Greater Good, 2003
Webber, Winni S. Lesbians in the Military Speak Out, 1993
Women's Research and Education Institute Women in the Military, Fourth Edition: Where They Stand, 2003
Dickinson Law Review ,Volume 102, Number 3, Spring 1998
Duke Journal of Gender Law & Policy, Volume 14, Issue 2, May 2007
The Georgetown Journal of Gender and the Law, Volume 2, Number 1, Fall 2000
The Georgetown Journal of Gender and the Law, Volume 3, Number 1, Fall 2001
The Georgetown Journal of Gender and the Law, Volume 2, Number 3, Summer 2001
Hastings Constitutional Law Quarterly, Volume 28, Number 2, Winter 2001
Hofstra Labor & Employment Law Journal, Volume 21, Number 2, Spring 2004
International Security, Volume 23, Number 2, Fall 1998
Journal of Gay, Lesbian and Bisexual Identity, Volume 3, Number 4, October 1998
New York University Law Review, Volume 70, Number 1, April 1995
Notre Dame Journal of Law, Ethics, and Public Policy, Volume 9, Number 1, 1995
UMKC Law Review, Volume 64, Number 1, Fall 1995

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: OutServe-SLDN Records, #7821. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
